_comment: Skeleton data file for /cabinet-refinishing-{town_slug}/ pages (Section 4.11 of the spec). Each entry must produce >=80-100% unique content per town to avoid doorway-page penalties. Placeholder fields are marked - replace before publishing. Neighbor towns are geographic approximations for internal-linking purposes; adjust if Jeremiah prefers different pairings.

towns.housing_stock_note: PLACEHOLDER - draft unique copy: Medford's mix of pre-1978 colonials and condo conversions near Tufts and the Mystic River means many kitchens still have original solid-wood cabinet boxes worth preserving rather than replacing.

towns.housing_stock_note: PLACEHOLDER - draft unique copy: Malden's dense mix of 2-6 unit multi-family triple-deckers and condo-converted units means cabinet refinishing is a fast, lower-disruption upgrade for both owner-occupants and investor-owners ahead of a tenant turnover.
